mnemonics.title: System Metrics Categories
mnemonics.content: CULND: CPU, Utilization, Latency, Network, Disk - five pillars of system metrics

mnemonics.title: Storytelling Framework
mnemonics.content: CCTV: Collect data, Contextualize, Tell the story, Validate findings - four steps to make stats tell stories

mnemonics.title: Threshold Levels
mnemonics.content: GOW: Green (0-60%), Orange (60-80%), Warning (80-90%), Red (90-100%) - utilization zones

mnemonics.title: Performance Indicators
mnemonics.content: LILA: Leading Indicators (predict), Lagging Indicators (confirm), Independent metrics, Linked metrics

acronyms.usage: Used to describe response time SLAs - P99 means 99% of requests must be faster than this value
Primary metric for compute capacity planning
Disk and network operations per second
Metrics that directly measure business objectives
Contractual performance commitments often measured via percentiles

visualTips.visualization: Draw an L-shaped curve: flat at low utilization, then sharp rise after 70-80% - shows why leaving headroom matters
Line graph showing metric over time with baseline (horizontal) and threshold (dotted line) - anomalies appear above threshold
Histogram showing response time distribution - tail on right shows outliers affecting P95/P99
Scatter plot with CPU on X-axis, Memory on Y-axis - points clustering show positive correlation

analogies.analogy: Like a stadium with limited seats - once full, people must wait in long queues outside
Like exam rankings - P95 means you scored better than 95% of students, not your actual marks
Your regular commute time - deviations tell you about traffic or accidents
Smoke detector (leading - warns before fire) vs Security camera (lagging - records after event)
